data.title: Bitwise CIO: Tariff storm may be good for BTC, maintains price target of $200,000 by the end of the year
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News Bitwise Chief Investment Officer Matt Hougan expects the tariff storm to be good for Bitcoin because the trade war will weaken the dollar's reserve currency status and stimulate demand for "hard currencies" such as Bitcoin. Despite escalating tensions, the asset management company still maintains its year-end BTC price target of $200,000. (The Block)

data.title: EU approves tariffs on 21 billion euros worth of U.S. goods
data.description: The EU approved additional tariffs on 21 billion euros of US goods in response to the metal dispute. (Jinshi)

data.title: EU: If a fair deal is reached with the United States, counter-tariffs can be suspended
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: The European Commission said it will take the first batch of countermeasures against US tariffs on April 15. The EU said it could suspend counter-tariffs if a fair deal is reached with the United States. (Jinshi)

data.title: U.S. Treasury Secretary Downplays Bond Sell-Off Amid Market Turmoil
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, U.S. Treasury Secretary Besent has downplayed concerns over the recent sell-off in U.S. Treasury bonds, stating that it is not a systemic issue. Besent noted that the market is currently experiencing a period of deleveraging, a situation he has frequently observed during his career in hedge funds. He emphasized that this is a characteristic of the fixed income market, where some heavily leveraged players are incurring losses and are forced to deleverage.

In recent days, long-term Treasury bonds have been at the forefront of declines in the U.S. bond market, despite concurrent drops in the stock market, which contradicts their typical role as a safe haven. So far this week, the yield on 30-year U.S. Treasury bonds has surged by approximately 0.5 percentage points. Besent remarked, "I do not believe this is a systemic issue. I think the deleveraging occurring in the bond market is a concerning yet normal process."

data.title: Fed's Kashkari Discusses Challenges in Adjusting Interest Rates Amid Tariff Policies
data.description: According to Odaily, Federal Reserve official Neel Kashkari stated that any adjustments to monetary policy, whether through raising or lowering interest rates, should not be ruled out. He noted that tariff policies have increased the obstacles to adjusting rates. Even if the economy and labor market weaken, the threshold for rate cuts remains higher.

data.title: U.S. Treasury Secretary Comments on Economic Stability Amid Trade Talks
data.description: According to Odaily, U.S. Treasury Secretary Besent stated that despite some uncertainties, businesses have conveyed that the economy remains robust. He noted that if other countries refrain from retaliatory actions, the current tariff levels will serve as the maximum. Numerous countries are eager to engage in discussions in Washington, with 70 negotiations already scheduled. Ultimately, agreements with allies may be reached.

data.title: Tether Plans to Launch AI Platform as Alternative to OpenAI
data.description: According to Odaily, Tether's CEO Paolo Ardoino has disclosed in a recent interview that while the company's U.S. stablecoin initiative is still in its early and uncertain stages, Tether is actively working on diversifying into artificial intelligence. The company plans to launch its own AI platform in June or September, which will serve as a peer-to-peer alternative to models like OpenAI. This platform will enable users to control their data and perform all reasoning and complex AI logic on their own devices, ranging from $30 smartphones to iPhones, Android phones, and any laptop. Additionally, it will allow direct connections to other devices for enhanced functionality, eliminating the need to share data with ChatGPT.

data.title: U.S. Treasury Secretary Benson: It is possible to reach an agreement with allies, and 70 negotiations are currently scheduled
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: U.S. Treasury Secretary Bessant said that although there is some uncertainty, businesses told me that the economy is very solid. If other countries do not take retaliatory measures, the tariff level will be the upper limit. A large number of countries hope to come to Washington for negotiations, and 70 negotiations are currently scheduled. In the end, we may reach an agreement with our allies. (Jinshi)

data.title: Traders see more than a 60% chance the Fed will start cutting rates in May
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: According to market news: Traders believe that the probability of the Federal Reserve starting to cut interest rates in May is more than 60%. (Jinshi)
